your_files: src/click/core.py (Option.__init__ batch_size attribute only),src/click/decorators.py or a new module for chunking logic
note: Both features need core.py, so let's split by region instead of by file to avoid clobbering. I will touch: Option.__init__ (add dynamic_default detection/validation of dict defaults), a new private resolution method on Option, Option.get_help_record (dynamic default help text), and Command.main (add a resolution pass after parsing/before validation). Please keep your batch_size changes to a separate, additive attribute in Option.__init__ (different lines) and avoid editing get_help_record/Command.main. If batch_size chunking logic doesn't need to live in Option itself, consider putting it in decorators.py or a new module to minimize overlap. Also I need types.py for ParamType default-formatting hooks - let me know if you also need it and for what, so we can carve out separate sections there too.

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py (Option.__init__ end-of-init __debug__ block + to_info_dict + new method after to_info_dict),src/click/decorators.py or new module
note: Agreed, this works. Confirming my exact edit points in core.py so we don't collide: (1) Option.__init__ - I'm adding a small block at the very TOP of the method body, before the existing 'default_is_missing = ...' line (~line 2557), to detect/validate dict-based defaults. I will NOT touch the __debug__ block at the end of __init__ (~lines 2640-2663) - that's yours for batch_size validation. (2) I will NOT touch to_info_dict at all - all yours for the batch_size key. (3) Any new helper method I add will go near get_help_extra/get_default (~line 2900+), not directly after to_info_dict - so your new method placed right after to_info_dict is safe. (4) I'm also adding a resolution pass inside Command.parse_args (~line 1196-1210, base Command class, not Group/main) - a new loop after the existing per-param processing loop. I won't touch invoke() or main(). Go ahead - this looks disjoint to me.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
note: Finished implementing dynamic default resolution. Changes confined to: (1) Command.parse_args - new second-pass loop after the existing per-param processing loop (before the extra-args check), (2) Option.__init__ - new validation/detection block right at the top before 'default_is_missing = ...', (3) Option.get_help_extra - small addition to the existing show_default branch for '(dynamic)' text, (4) new Option.resolve_dynamic_default method placed right after get_default, before prompt_for_value. Did not touch to_info_dict, the end-of-__init__ __debug__ block, or types.py. Tests pass (680 passed in the existing suite, unrelated pre-existing collection error in test_basic.py from a pytest version issue, confirmed present on a clean checkout too). Patch written to patch.txt. All yours to proceed/finish up.
